message:
fix(install): warn before auto-installing gum (beads_rust-dhzk)

try_install_gum() previously installed charmbracelet/gum silently
(all output to /dev/null) via package managers or GitHub download,
potentially invoking sudo without any user notice. Add a one-line
stderr warning before the install attempt so the user knows what is
happening and can pass --no-gum to skip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -159,6 +159,8 @@ try_install_gum() {
     [[ -z "${CI:-}" ]] || return 1
     [[ -t 1 ]] || return 1
 
+    printf >&2 '%s\n' "Note: installing 'gum' (charmbracelet/gum) for styled output. Pass --no-gum to skip."
+
     # Inline OS detection
     local os="unknown"
     case "$(uname -s)" in
